summary: Reviewed the inbox runner and paired delegate together as an end-to-end readonly smoke path. Verdict: needs_revision. The pair shows strong evidence-backed and readonly-oriented design, but the wrapper has a material contract mismatch: it can report a non-success wrapper status even when the delegate exits 0, and it does not treat delegate non-zero exit codes with available JSON evidence as a hard failure signal. This weakens end-to-end execution semantics and review confidence.
notes: Review grounded in supplied artifact snapshots for both scripts.
Evaluated wrapper and delegate together per requested paired review scope.
Generated required markdown review artifact content and included metadata.verdict.
